name: Flipkart Order Dashboard — N+1 Detection & Fix
scenario: The Flipkart order dashboard is slow in production. 10,000 orders × 2 associations = 20,001 database queries per request. Identify the N+1, apply the correct fix (JOIN FETCH / @EntityGraph / @BatchSize as appropriate), write query-count assertion tests, and measure the improvement.
description: Order @Entity with User @ManyToOne and Product @ManyToOne associations. N+1 demonstration, 3 fix approaches (JOIN FETCH, @EntityGraph, DTO projection), and @DataJpaTest verifying exactly 1 query using Hibernate statistics.

core.description: Order @Entity: id (UUID), user @ManyToOne (LAZY), product @ManyToOne (LAZY), status VARCHAR, quantity INT, total DECIMAL, created_at. User @Entity: id, name, email. Product @Entity: id, name, category, price.
OrderService.getAllOrderDtos() version A — the N+1 version using findAll() + accessing associations in loop. Document with comments showing WHERE N+1 happens and how many queries for 100 orders.

OrderRepository with 3 methods: (1) findAllWithDetails() using JOIN FETCH user + product, (2) @EntityGraph version on findByStatus(), (3) DTO projection findSummaryByStatus() returning OrderSummaryDto(orderId, userName, productName, total).
OrderService.getAllOrderDtos() version B — uses findAllWithDetails() JOIN FETCH. getAllOrderSummaries(status) uses the DTO projection.

@DataJpaTest with Hibernate statistics (4 tests): (1) getAllOrderDtos() returns correct count, (2) getAllOrderDtos() query count == 1, (3) getAllOrderSummaries() query count == 1, (4) user name correct in response. All 4 pass.
README in project: shows before (N+1 log with repeated queries) vs after (single JOIN query) using the spring.jpa.show-sql output. Explains why the query count went from 2N+1 to 1.

bonus.description: Add Order @OneToMany List<OrderItem> items (lazy, @BatchSize=25). Show that findAllWithDetails() still uses 1 query for user+product, and OrderItem loading uses batched IN queries (not N+1). @DataJpaTest verifying batch behavior.

Add pagination: Page<Order> findByStatus(String status, Pageable pageable) with @EntityGraph. Verify it does NOT cause HHH90003004 (no @OneToMany JOIN FETCH with Pageable). 1 query for data, 1 query for count = 2 total.
Add @NamedEntityGraph on Order for 'Order.forDashboard' (user + product) and 'Order.forDetailView' (user + product + items). Use named graphs in repository methods.

data_to_use:
Seed for @DataJpaTest:
  Users: Aisha Sharma (aisha@test.com), Raj Patel (raj@test.com)
  Products: Wireless Mouse (Electronics, ₹799), Keyboard (Electronics, ₹1999)
  Orders:
    - Order 1: Aisha, Mouse, PLACED, qty=1, total=799
    - Order 2: Aisha, Keyboard, DELIVERED, qty=1, total=1999
    - Order 3: Raj, Mouse, PLACED, qty=2, total=1598

Expected query counts:
  N+1 (broken): 1 + 3 + 3 = 7 queries (findAll + user×3 + product×3)
  JOIN FETCH (fixed): 1 query
  DTO projection: 1 query
example_output:
# Before fix (show-sql output for 3 orders):
Hibernate: select id,user_id,product_id,status,total from orders
Hibernate: select id,name,email from users where id='U001'
Hibernate: select id,name,price from products where id='P001'
Hibernate: select id,name,email from users where id='U001'  ← DUPLICATE
Hibernate: select id,name,price from products where id='P002'
Hibernate: select id,name,email from users where id='U002'
Hibernate: select id,name,price from products where id='P001'  ← DUPLICATE
Total: 7 queries for 3 orders (2N+1)

# After JOIN FETCH fix:
Hibernate: select o.id,o.status,o.total,
           u.id,u.name,u.email,
           p.id,p.name,p.price
           from orders o
           inner join users u on o.user_id=u.id
           inner join products p on o.product_id=p.id
Total: 1 query for 3 orders

# Tests:
✓ getAllOrderDtos_returnsCorrectCount
✓ getAllOrderDtos_exactlyOneQuery
✓ getAllOrderSummaries_exactlyOneQuery
✓ getAllOrderDtos_correctUserName

criteria: Order entity with LAZY associations (8 pts)

ai_policy_explanation: Limited to 5 hints. testEm.clear() mandatory before query count assertion — without it, L1 cache hides N+1. JOIN FETCH on multiple @ManyToOne: JOIN FETCH o.user JOIN FETCH o.product (space-separated, same @Query string). DTO projection @Query: SELECT new com.package.Dto(t.field, t.user.name) FROM Order t — nested field access is supported. sf.getStatistics().getPreparedStatementCount(): call clear() first, then your query, then assert.
submission_instructions: GitHub push. All 4 @DataJpaTest tests pass. README includes before/after SQL log screenshots (or copy-paste). spring.jpa.show-sql=true in test config.
tips_for_success: Seed all entities in @BeforeEach, call testEm.clear() at the END of @BeforeEach
sf.getStatistics().clear() immediately before the method call, not before seeding
JOIN FETCH with two @ManyToOne: space between — 'JOIN FETCH o.user JOIN FETCH o.product'
DTO projection: fully qualified class name in new ClassName(...) in JPQL
@EntityGraph on findByStatus(): Spring adds LEFT JOIN — different from JOIN FETCH INNER JOIN
concepts_reinforced: N+1 pattern identification in code and logs
Hibernate statistics for query count assertion
L1 cache eviction with testEm.clear() before tests
